title: Sarepta (SRPT) Q1 2024 Earnings Beat Estimates - Revenues Up Year-Over-Year
text:
Sarepta Therapeutics reported strong Q1 2024 results, beating analyst expectations on both earnings and revenue metrics. Sarepta Therapeutics reported earnings per share of 44 cents, which was above the analyst estimate for EPS of 2 cents. The company reported revenue of $413.46 million, which was 10.77% better than the analyst estimate for revenue of $373.28 million.

Net Product Revenue: Total net product revenue reached $360 million, representing 55% growth year-over-year. Product breakdown: ELEVIDYS contributed $133.9 million. PMO franchise contributed $225.5 million, relatively flat compared to Q1 2023.

ELEVIDYS Progress: Sales have plateaued as anticipated while awaiting label expansion approval. Over $334 million in cumulative sales since June 2023 approval. The company was in quiet period regarding ELEVIDYS BLA supplement seeking to remove age and ambulation restrictions, with expected receipt of draft label shortly.

PMO Franchise Details: EXONDYS 51: $120.2 million. AMONDYS 45: $71.9 million. VYONDYS 53: $33.5 million. The three-drug platform remained relatively stable despite gene therapy competition.

Pipeline and R&D: EMERGENE trial (SRP-9003 for LGMD2E) enrollment has begun. Real-world evidence published showing eteplirsen users survived 5.4 years longer than untreated patients. SRP-5051 peptide-conjugated PMO showed strong dystrophin production results.

Financial Position: Company maintains approximately $1.4 billion in cash and investments, positioning them to support potential label expansion and ongoing clinical development.
